description:
First subticket of fz-ul4.18.

Add Elixir-style modules to fz. Top-level grammar grows a new item:

  defmodule Name do
    fn f(x), do: x + 1
    defmacro m(x), do: quote do: unquote(x)
  end

## Scope

- Lexer: `defmodule` keyword (Tok::Defmodule).
- Parser: top-level item `defmodule UpperName do <items> end`. Body items are fn/defmacro only in this subticket (nested defmodule is .18.2).
- AST: `Item::Module(ModuleDef { name, items })`. Existing top-level fns (no enclosing defmodule) remain valid — they live in the bare global namespace as today.
- Qualified calls: `Mod.fn(args)` parses as `Expr::Call(Expr::Dot(Var("Mod"), "fn"), args)`. Already lexes today; just needs the call-target Dot path through the resolver.
- Name resolution pass between parse and macro-expansion:
  - Walk modules; for each, collect its local fn/macro names.
  - Inside the module's bodies, rewrite bare `Var(n)` (and `Call(Var(n), args)`) where n names a sibling -> qualified `Var("Mod.n")`.
  - Rewrite `Call(Dot(Var("M"), "n"), args)` -> `Call(Var("M.n"), args)` for any module path that resolves.
  - Outside modules, no rewrite.
- Eval: globals env stays flat. load_program registers each module's items under qualified "Mod.n" names.
- AOT/JIT: monomorphization works on user_name as before — just longer names.
- Macros expansion-time interp: same as today since macros are now in interp.globals under their qualified names.

## Tests

- Single module: `defmodule M do; fn f(x), do: x + 1 end` + `fn main() do M.f(41) end` -> 42.
- Two modules sharing a fn name: `defmodule A do; fn helper(x), do: x + 1 end` + `defmodule B do; fn helper(x), do: x * 2 end` + main calling both. No collision.
- Sibling call within module: `defmodule M do; fn helper(x), do: x + 1; fn use_helper(x), do: helper(x) end`. Bare `helper` resolves to M.helper.
- Existing un-modular fixtures still pass.

## Out of scope

- Nested defmodule (.18.2)
- alias / import (.18.3 / .18.4)
- Cross-module macros with module-aware expansion (.18.5)
- Module attributes (.18.6)
title: Core defmodule + qualified calls
